title: Biscuit Baron
content:
{{Top|ffa|leg}} {{Youmay|the [[The galaxy/Legends|galactic]] [[fast food]] chain|the [[Biscuit Barren]] on [[Port Tooga]]|linebreak=1}} {{Company |image=[[File:Biscuit_Baron.svg]] |name=Biscuit Baron |founder= |ledby=[[Roridan Temquill]] {{C|[[Chief Executive Officer/Legends|Chief Executive Officer]] - [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]]}}{{HoloNetNewsWeb|49|business/13328_2.html|Biscuit Baron to Celebrate "Heroes of the Republic"}} |associations= |parent=[[Tagge Restaurant Association/Legends|Tagge Restaurant Association]]''[[Han Solo and the Corporate Sector Sourcebook]]'' and ''[[The Far Orbit Project]]'' |subsid= |headquarters= |locations=*[[Byblos/Legends|Byblos]] [[Byblos Starport|Starport]]''[[Platt's Starport Guide]]'' *[[Commenor/Legends|Commenor]]{{HoloNetNewsWeb|54|regional/1352_2.html|Hunger Pangs Start to Hit The Colonies}} *[[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]]''[[Coruscant and the Core Worlds]]'' *[[Sullust/Legends|Sullust]]{{JournalCite|11|Bungo n' Rusti Get Carry-Out}} |role=[[Fast food]] chain |products=*[[Bantha Breakfast Biscuit]]s *[[Blue sauce]] *[[Jolly Meal]]{{InsiderCite|89|Underworld: A Galaxy of Scum and Villainy}} *[[QuickSnack]] *[[QuickSnackLite]] *[[Ardees/Legends|Ardees]] |founding= |dissolved= |affiliation=[[Tagge Company/Legends|Tagge Company]] }} {{Quote|If you want juicy conversation and a hot cup of ardees, come to Dexter's Diner. If you want a ''good'' cup of ardees, try Biscuit Baron.|Slyther Bushforb|}} '''Biscuit Baron''' was a dining establishment owned by the [[House of Tagge/Legends|House of Tagge]] through the [[Tagge Company/Legends|Tagge Company's]] subsidiary [[Tagge Restaurant Association/Legends|Tagge Restaurant Association]]. With stores located on several [[Planet/Legends|planets]] throughout [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]], Biscuit Baron was, by the time of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire's]] prominence, the largest restaurant chain in existence. [[Sullust/Legends|Sullust]] possessed one such establishment, as did [[Byblos/Legends|Byblos]] [[Byblos Starport|Starport]], [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]], and [[Commenor/Legends|Commenor]]. Among the foodstuffs sold was the [[Bantha Breakfast Biscuit]], its signature [[blue sauce]], the alcoholic beverage [[Ardees/Legends|ardees]], and meal offers such as a [[QuickSnack]], [[QuickSnackLite]], and [[Jolly Meal]]. Non-edible products included [[spot-on locator]]s—modified to announce the nearest Biscuit Baron—and, for a month during the events leading up to the [[Separatist Crisis/Legends|Separatist Crisis]] and the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], commemorative [[Holocube/Legends|holocubes]] representing key members in [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Republic]] history. The Bantha Breakfast Biscuit also played a major role in the crisis surrounding the planet [[Gastrula]]'s demise, in the form of the [[Giant Amorphous Bantha Breakfast Biscuit]], a creature formed by the combination of the foodstuff's ingredients and the atmosphere of Gastrula. ==Description== [[File:BiscuitBaron_Sullust.jpg|thumb|left|A Biscuit Baron restaurant on Sullust]] Biscuit Baron was a food establishment which offered a wide variety of foodstuffs. The establishments were located on several colonized [[Planet/Legends|worlds]], including the galactic [[Capital/Legends|capital]] of [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]], as well as the planets [[Sullust/Legends|Sullust]], [[Commenor/Legends|Commenor]], and [[Byblos/Legends|Byblos]]. During the time of [[Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine's]] reign of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], Biscuit Baron was the largest restaurant chain in [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]]. Even so, there were places that Biscuit Baron's influence had not reached; [[Gastrula]] was one such place. Entrepreneur [[Bungo Bung]], a [[Sullustan/Legends|Sullustan]], exploited this fact by running his own delivery service between these planets and Sullust, earning a large profit by selling [[Bantha Breakfast Biscuit]]s and their complementary [[blue sauce]]. Unfortunately for the people of Gastrula, one of the planets on Bung's run, the planet's unique atmosphere combined with the ingredients of the product to create a [[Giant Amorphous Bantha Breakfast Biscuit|being]] that devoured the entire planet. The restaurant was named for the [[Baron/Legends|title held by]] the leading member of the [[House of Tagge/Legends|House of Tagge]]. A stylized logo of Baron [[Orman Tagge/Legends|Orman Tagge]], chairman of [[Tagge Company/Legends|Tagge Company]], graced both signs at storefronts and crates of goods sold by the establishment; this logo was designed after Tagge lost his sight in [[18 BBY/Legends|18 BBY]], as it depicts him wearing his trademark goggles.''[[Evasive Action: End Game]]'' The logo bore the name of the restaurant in the [[High Galactic/Legends|High Galactic alphabet]]. The Tagge Company owned the subsidiary [[Tagge Restaurant Association/Legends|Tagge Restaurant Association]] which in turn controlled the Biscuit Baron restaurants. During the [[Separatist Crisis/Legends|Separatist Crisis]], the [[Chief Executive Officer/Legends|Chief Executive Officer]] of Biscuit Baron was [[Roridan Temquill]]; it was also during this time that a special "[[Heroes of the Republic (campaign)|Heroes of the Republic]]" promotion was released. Attendants at the Biscuit Baron located on the [[Colonies/Legends|Colonies]] world of Byblos, wore [[Hat/Legends|hats]] and collared short-sleeved [[shirt]]s. Customers visiting a Biscuit Baron were not required to wear formal [[Clothing/Legends|dress]]; this facet of dining at the restaurant was used to describe beings wearing particularly nondescript attire, as [[Baron Administrator/Legends|Baron Administrator]] [[Lando Calrissian/Legends|Lando Calrissian]] said of [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] [[Thune (bounty hunter)|Thune]].{{Hyperspace|url=hyperspace/member/fiction/f20080522/index.html|text="Lando Calrissian: Idiot's Array"|int=Lando Calrissian: Idiot's Array|newhost=Suvudu}} [[Airspeeder/Legends|Airspeeder]] pick-up lanes were common to Biscuit Baron establishments, allowing customers quick access to the food without leaving the comfort of their vehicle. The speakers mounted in such lanes were notorious for their bad quality.{{Wizards|url=default.asp?x=starwars/article/sw20030724takedown|text=Zygerrian Takedown|int=Zygerrian Takedown}} On Sullust, the Biscuit Baron building was cylindrical in shape and topped by a large dome; this dome possessed a large High Galactic Alphabet "B" as a way of identification. Further identification was provided in the form of a large sign bearing the Biscuit Baron logo located outside the main structure—this sign too bore the High Galactic Alphabet. Outside was parking for several [[Landspeeder/Legends|landspeeders]] around the perimeter of the facility. [[Byblos Starport Tower 214]], an immense structure on the Byblos boasting a vast number of amenities, contained a Biscuit Baron branch on each of its 300-plus levels. Each individual branch itself contained several kitchens and an expansive dining area. These restaurants often attracted [[Bulk freighter/Legends|bulk freighter]] crews with little money to spend, as well as the newer [[Captain/Legends|captains]]. Also, modified [[spot-on locator]]s were given away by Biscuit Baron; these customized units detailed the distance from the nearest Biscuit Baron in addition to their standard features. ==Products== [[File:BungoBung.png|thumb|right|Bungo Bung enjoying a Bantha Breakfast Biscuit]] {{Quote|Being a droid, Rusti, you fail to realize how good this is! You could compare it to the best oil bath you've ever had.''"
"''Really.|Bungo Bung praises the Bantha Breakfast Biscuit|}} Biscuit Baron offered several unique menu items to patrons, including Bantha Breakfast Biscuits. Blue sauce, a condiment which enhanced the flavor of the breakfast biscuits, was served alongside. [[Jolly Meal]]s were another product sold; its reputation in the galaxy was large enough for it to be used in even official documents as a descriptor. [[QuickSnack]]s and [[QuickSnackLite]]s were served around the beginning of the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]] in [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]]; during the same time period a special promotional deal was offered alongside the respective food bundles. Biscuit Barons served [[Ardees/Legends|ardees]], an alcoholic beverage; the restaurant had the reputation for selling a good cup of ardees, noted by [[Private investigator/Legends|private investigator]] [[Slyther Bushforb/Legends|Slyther Bushforb]]. In the time surrounding the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], the food was so cheap, especially compared to alternatives, that five [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]] could buy a veritable "feast." According to at least one guide, however, the food itself was bland. Biscuit Baron's edible products, particularly the Bantha Breakfast Biscuit, possessed a long shelf life: one crate of the product lasted through Imperial detainment and the [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] travel between Sullust and Gastrula. Many species were able to obtain products sold at Biscuit Baron, including Sullustans, [[Wookiee/Legends|Wookiees]], [[Rodian/Legends|Rodians]], and [[Arcona/Legends|Arcona]]. ===Bantha Breakfast Biscuit=== {{Main|Bantha Breakfast Biscuit|Giant Amorphous Bantha Breakfast Biscuit}} In addition to its remarkable longevity, the Bantha Breakfast Biscuit was also notable for an incident on Gastrula. Certain preservatives in the Biscuit reacted with the stale air of a starship and the Gastrulan atmosphere, causing it to experience a metamorphosis that, when complete, resulted in the former foodstuff becoming the semi-sentient creature known as the Giant Amorphous Bantha Breakfast Biscuit. This creature was shown capable of eating entire planets (as it had Gastrula), as well as the hull plating and other systems of a cargo barge. The giant being, however, was able to be defeated by copious amounts of blue sauce, the application of which resulted in the being's demise. ===Promotional deals=== {{Quote|The goal is to educate young people, all people really, about the heroes and history of the last thousand years. It's a celebration of everything the Republic is and can be.|CEO Roridan Temquill, [[HoloNet News Vol. 531 49|"Biscuit Baron to Celebrate 'Heroes of the Republic'"]]|}} During the events leading up to the Clone Wars, Biscuit Baron establishments around the galaxy offered a series of commemorative [[Holocube/Legends|holocubes]] with QuickSnack and QuickSnackLite meals in accordance with their "Heroes of the Republic" campaign. While targeted towards the younger members of the galaxy, CEO Roridan Temquill acknowledged that it was hoped that all people would learn from the included informational holocubes. These holocubes depicted notable historical figures within the Republic, such as [[Supreme Chancellor/Legends|Supreme Chancellors]] [[Anwis Eddicus]] and [[Vaila Pecivas]]. Certain [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Jedi Master/Legends|Masters]], including [[S'diawae]], [[Ikrit]], and [[Jojighar]], were also featured. The promotion lasted [[Galactic Standard Calendar|one standard month]] and differed slightly from region to region. Spot-on locators, whose use included pathfinding through unfamiliar terrain, were given out by Biscuit Baron establishments on heavily populated worlds. These location units, modified from the stock model, would call out the kilometers to the nearest Biscuit Baron. ==History== [[File:Byblos_Starport_Biscuit_Baron.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Patrons line up to purchase food at a Biscuit Baron in Byblos Starport]] Biscuit Baron was owned by the Tagge Company, under its Tagge Restaurant Association subsidiary, as early as 22 BBY. During this time, the company's CEO was Roridan Temquill. It was also during this time that Biscuit Barons began offering promotional holocubes, under the title "Heroes of the Republic," in an effort to teach about key figures in Republic history. By the time period leading up to and continuing into the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], around [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]], Biscuit Baron was the largest dining establishment in existence in the galaxy. Its appearance in the galaxy was so commonplace that colloquial references to it were made in official documents. Within [[Shoola's Ring]] on the [[Space station/Legends|space station]] [[Port Tooga]], a [[Chef/Legends|chef]] was inspired by an archway playing a nonsense message that ended with the sounds "bikkik barran." The chef rented the associated space and launched [[Biscuit Barren]], a dining establishment that appropriated the name and iconography of Biscuit Baron. Biscuit Barren developed a better reputation than that of the TaggeCo chain, particularly for its breakfast sandwiches and shakes.{{FFG|story=Dead in the Water|book=Star Wars: Age of Rebellion Game Master's Kit|text=''Star Wars: Age of Rebellion'' Game Master's Kit}} ==Behind the scenes== The concept of a galaxy-wide restaurant chain was initially brought forth in the ''[[Han Solo and the Corporate Sector Sourcebook]]'' written by [[Michael Allen Horne]] and published in [[1993]], where it was realized in the form of Biscuit Baron. After that, Biscuit Baron was name-dropped in various sources until its first appearance in the ''[[Bungo n' Rusti Get Carry-Out]]'' comic, written by [[James Anderson|Jim Anderson]], featured in ''[[Star Wars Adventure Journal]]'' [[Star Wars Adventure Journal 11|11]] in [[1996]]. It would go unmentioned until [[Pablo Hidalgo]] and [[Paul Ens]]'s work on the [[HoloNet News (original website)|HoloNet News]] entries published in [[2002]]. After this, a sourcebook, as well as several other sources, have used the name. ==Appearances== *{{Wizards|url=default.asp?x=starwars/article/sw20030724takedown|text=Zygerrian Takedown|int=Zygerrian Takedown}} {{Mo}} *{{HoloNetNewsWeb|49|business/13328_2.html|Biscuit Baron to Celebrate "Heroes of the Republic"}} *{{HoloNetNewsWeb|54|regional/1352_2.html|Hunger Pangs Start to Hit The Colonies}} *''[[Evasive Action: End Game]]'' *{{JournalCite|11|Bungo n' Rusti Get Carry-Out}} {{1st}} *{{FFG|story=Dead in the Water|book=Star Wars: Age of Rebellion Game Master's Kit|text=''Star Wars: Age of Rebellion'' Game Master's Kit}} {{Mo}} *{{Hyperspace|url=hyperspace/member/fiction/f20080522/index.html|text="Lando Calrissian: Idiot's Array"|int=Lando Calrissian: Idiot's Array|newhost=Suvudu}} {{Mo}} ==Sources== *''[[Han Solo and the Corporate Sector Sourcebook]]'' {{1stm}} *{{JournalCite|2|Rebel Privateers!|reprint=1}} *''[[Platt's Starport Guide]]'' *''[[Pirates & Privateers]]'' *''[[The Far Orbit Project]]'' *''[[The New Essential Guide to Characters]]'' *{{Wizards|url=default.asp?x=starwars/article/sw20021017web|text=''Arms & Equipment Guide'' Extras!}} *''[[Coruscant and the Core Worlds]]'' *''[[The New Essential Guide to Droids]]'' *{{InsiderCite|89|Underworld: A Galaxy of Scum and Villainy}} *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |nl=Biscuit Baron }} [[Category:Fast food establishments]] [[Category:Foodstuff manufacturers]] [[Category:TaggeCo subsidiaries]]
